_meta.project: Maryland Open Platform (MOP) — UMD Smith School
_meta.note: This file is the primary knowledge source for the NL-to-SQL agent. Keep it current as datasets are updated.
_meta.seventh_dataset: Agency-level federal spending: contract_state_agency, contract_county_agency, contract_cd_agency

state_name_casing.problem: State names are stored in different cases across tables. JOINs will silently fail without normalization.
state_name_casing.sql_fix: Always wrap state in LOWER() before joining: LOWER(a.state) = LOWER(b.state)
state_name_casing.example: SELECT a.state, a.financial_literacy, b.Contracts FROM finra_state a JOIN contract_state b ON LOWER(a.state) = LOWER(b.state) WHERE a.Year = 2021 AND b.year = '2024'

special_character_columns.problem: These column names contain commas and ampersands and MUST be double-quoted in all SQL

columns_with_spaces.problem: Several columns in contract and spending tables contain spaces and must be quoted

year_as_string_in_contract_tables.problem: The year column in contract_state, contract_county, contract_congress, and spending_state_agency is a STRING, not an integer. Values are '2024' or '2020-2024' (5-year aggregate).
year_as_string_in_contract_tables.sql_fix: Filter with: WHERE year = '2024'  (not WHERE year = 2024)

gov_year_label.note: The Year column in gov_state, gov_county, gov_congress contains string value 'Fiscal Year 2023', not an integer. The data is single-year (FY2023 only).
gov_year_label.sql_fix: Do not filter by year for gov tables — there is only one year of data.

finra_years_available.note: finra_state has data for survey years 2009, 2012, 2015, 2018, 2021. finra_county and finra_congress only have 2021.

state_flow_duplicate_columns.problem: In state_flow, rcpt_st_cd and rcpt_state_name contain identical data (both are full state names). Likewise subawardee_st_cd = subawardee_state_name. Always use the *_state_name versions for clarity.
state_flow_duplicate_columns.fix: Use rcpt_state_name and subawardee_state_name in all queries on state_flow

congress_flow_integer_district_id.problem: congress_flow.prime_awardee_stcd118 and subawardee_stcd118 are INTEGER codes, not the 'MD-08' string format used in all other congress-level tables. Joining these to cd_118 in other tables will produce wrong results.
congress_flow_integer_district_id.fix: Filter congress_flow by rcpt_cd_name (e.g. WHERE rcpt_cd_name = 'Maryland CD-08') or by rcpt_state. Never join prime_awardee_stcd118 to cd_118 columns.

county_flow_fips_as_integer.note: county_flow.rcpt_cty and subawardee_cty are integer FIPS codes — safe to join directly with gov_county.fips and acs_county.fips

contract_county_agency_state_aggregate_r.problem: contract_county_agency has rows where county_fips = 1 (or single-digit values) — these are state-level aggregate rows, not real counties.
contract_county_agency_state_aggregate_r.fix: Always add WHERE county_fips > 1000 when querying contract_county_agency for county-level results

state_identifier.description: Full U.S. state name. Case varies by table — always use LOWER() when joining. See _critical_warnings.state_name_casing.

county_identifier.description: County name without 'County' suffix. Lowercase in most tables, UPPERCASE in contract tables.
county_identifier.join_key: fips is the reliable join key between county-level tables — prefer fips over county name string matching

fips_identifier.description: Federal Information Processing Standards code — unique 4-5 digit integer identifying each US county. Reliable cross-table join key.

congressional_district_identifier.description: 118th Congressional District code in format STATE_ABBR-DISTRICT_NUMBER. e.g. 'MD-08', 'CA-12', 'AK-00' (at-large). Fixed column name across all congress-level tables.

acs_state.description: American Community Survey (ACS) demographic data aggregated at the U.S. state level. Covers population, race, education, income, poverty, and housing.
acs_state.source: U.S. Census Bureau — American Community Survey
acs_state.grain: One row per state per year

White.description: Percentage of population identifying as White alone

Black.description: Percentage of population identifying as Black or African American alone

Asian.description: Percentage of population identifying as Asian alone

Hispanic.description: Percentage of population identifying as Hispanic or Latino (any race)

Education >= High School.description: Percentage of adults 25+ with at least a high school diploma or GED

Education >= Bachelor's.description: Percentage of adults 25+ with at least a bachelor's degree

Education >= Graduate.description: Percentage of adults 25+ with a graduate or professional degree

Income >$50K.description: Percentage of households with annual income above $50,000

Income >$100K.description: Percentage of households with annual income above $100,000

Income >$200K.description: Percentage of households with annual income above $200,000

Median household income.description: Median annual household income in USD

Below poverty.description: Percentage of population living below the federal poverty line

Owner occupied.description: Percentage of housing units that are owner-occupied

Renter occupied.description: Percentage of housing units that are renter-occupied

acs_county.description: ACS demographic data at the U.S. county level. Same variables as acs_state.
acs_county.source: U.S. Census Bureau — American Community Survey
acs_county.grain: One row per county per year

county.description: County name, lowercase, without 'County' suffix. e.g. 'montgomery', 'prince george's'

fips.description: FIPS county code — reliable join key across tables

acs_congress.description: ACS demographic data at the 118th Congressional District level. Same variables as acs_state/acs_county.
acs_congress.source: U.S. Census Bureau — American Community Survey
acs_congress.grain: One row per congressional district per year

cd_118.description: Congressional district code. Format: STATE_ABBR-DD. e.g. 'MD-08'

gov_state.description: Municipal government finance data aggregated at the U.S. state level. Covers balance sheet items, revenue, expenses, debt, pension liability, and derived ratios. Data is for Fiscal Year 2023 only — do not filter by year.
gov_state.source: U.S. Census Bureau — Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances
gov_state.grain: One row per state (single year: FY2023)
gov_state.year_note: Single year only — Fiscal Year 2023. Year column contains string 'Fiscal Year 2023'.

Total_Liabilities.description: Total government liabilities (all obligations) in USD

Current_Assets.description: Short-term assets convertible to cash within one year

Non-Current_Liabilities.description: Long-term obligations due beyond one year. Must be quoted in SQL.

Net_Position.description: Total assets minus total liabilities. Negative = fiscal stress.

Free_Cash_Flow.description: Operating cash flow minus capital expenditures

Bonds,_Loans_&_Notes.description: Outstanding debt from bonds, loans, and notes. MUST be quoted in SQL.

Net_Pension_Liability.description: Unfunded pension obligations owed to government employees. Large values signal long-term fiscal risk.

Net_OPEB_Liability.description: Net Other Post-Employment Benefits liability — unfunded healthcare and other benefits promised to retirees

Compensated_Absences.description: Liability for earned but unused employee leave (vacation, sick days) owed as future cash outflows

POPULATION.description: State population used for per-capita calculations

Current_Ratio.description: Current Assets / Current Liabilities. Value > 1 means government can cover short-term obligations. Value < 1 signals liquidity risk.

Debt_Ratio.description: Total Liabilities / Total Assets. Higher = more leveraged. Value > 1 means liabilities exceed assets.

Year.description: Always 'Fiscal Year 2023'. Do NOT filter by this column — it has only one value.

gov_county.description: Municipal government finance data at the U.S. county level. Same variables as gov_state. Single year: FY2023.
gov_county.source: U.S. Census Bureau — Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances
gov_county.grain: One row per county (single year: FY2023)

gov_congress.description: Municipal government finance data at the 118th Congressional District level. Same variables as gov_state. Single year: FY2023.
gov_congress.source: U.S. Census Bureau — Annual Survey of State and Local Government Finances
gov_congress.grain: One row per congressional district (single year: FY2023)

contract_state.description: Federal contract and grant awards aggregated at the U.S. state level. Two rows per state: one for 2024 only, one for the 2020-2024 five-year aggregate. Source: USAspending.gov.
contract_state.grain: One row per state per year-period
contract_state.year_type: VARCHAR (not integer — always filter with quoted string e.g. year = '2024')

year.description: Year or period. Values are '2024' or '2020-2024'. Always quote in SQL: WHERE year = '2024'

Contracts.description: Total federal contract obligations awarded to entities in this state

Grants.description: Total federal grant awards to entities in this state

Resident Wage.description: Total federal wages paid to residents of this state

Direct Payments.description: Federal direct payment transfers (e.g. Social Security, Medicare, benefits)

Federal Residents.description: Count of federal employees residing in this state

Employees.description: Federal civilian employees working in this state

Employees Wage.description: Total wages paid to federal employees working in this state

contract_county.description: Federal contract and grant awards at the U.S. county level. Same variables as contract_state minus Employees columns.
contract_county.grain: One row per county per year-period

county_fips.description: FIPS county code — use for joins

contract_congress.description: Federal contract and grant awards at the 118th Congressional District level.
contract_congress.grain: One row per congressional district per year-period

finra_state.description: FINRA National Financial Capability Study (NFCS) — financial health indices aggregated at the state level. All metric columns are normalized 0–1 indices (higher = more of that characteristic). Survey data: 2009, 2012, 2015, 2018, 2021 only.
finra_state.grain: One row per state per survey year

state.description: Full state name, Title Case. e.g. 'Maryland'. Use LOWER() when joining with other tables.

Year.description: Survey wave year. Only 2009, 2012, 2015, 2018, 2021 exist — do not filter for other years.

financial_constraint.description: Index (0–1) measuring household financial stress: liquidity pressure, difficulty covering expenses, lack of emergency savings. Higher = more constrained/stressed.

alternative_financing.description: Index (0–1) measuring reliance on high-cost non-traditional credit: payday loans, pawn shops, rent-to-own. Higher = more reliance on predatory/alternative finance.

financial_literacy.description: Index (0–1) measuring objective financial knowledge: interest compounding, inflation, bond prices, mortgage math. Higher = more financially literate.

satisfied.description: Share (0–1) of respondents satisfied with their overall financial condition. Higher = more satisfaction.

risk_averse.description: Share (0–1) of respondents with low willingness to take financial risk. Higher = more risk-averse population.

finra_county.description: FINRA NFCS financial health indices at the U.S. county level. 2021 only. Same metrics as finra_state.

state.description: Full state name, lowercase. Use LOWER() when joining.

financial_constraint.description: Household financial stress index (0–1). Higher = more stressed.

financial_literacy.description: Objective financial knowledge index (0–1). Higher = more literate.

risk_averse.description: Share unwilling to take financial risk (0–1)

finra_congress.description: FINRA NFCS financial health indices at the 118th Congressional District level. 2021 only.
finra_congress.grain: One row per congressional district (2021 only)

spending_state.description: Federal spending breakdown by state and spending category. Same structure as contract_state but focused on spending type breakdown. Two rows per state: 2024 and 2020-2024 aggregate.
spending_state.grain: One row per state per year-period

Year.description: Year or period string. Values: '2024' or '2020-2024'. Always quote.

spending_state_agency.description: Federal spending broken down by both state AND federal agency. Allows questions like 'how much did the Department of Defense spend in Maryland'. 15 agencies × 50 states × 2 year-periods.
spending_state_agency.grain: One row per agency per state per year-period
agency_values: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development

agency.description: Federal agency name. See agency_values for exact strings to use in WHERE clauses.
sample_values: Department of Health and Human Services

state_flow.description: Federal subaward fund flow data between U.S. states. Each row represents a dollar flow from a prime awardee state to a sub-awardee state, broken down by federal agency and NAICS industry. Use for questions about which states send or receive the most federal subaward money and through which agencies.
state_flow.grain: One row per prime-state → sub-state flow per agency per NAICS industry
state_flow.geography: state-to-state flow (no year column — annual amounts embedded in subaward_amount_year)
critical_notes: rcpt_st_cd and rcpt_state_name are DUPLICATES — both contain the same full state name in Title Case. Use rcpt_state_name for clarity.
Unnamed: 0 is a row index — never use in queries.
subaward_amount_year can be NEGATIVE (represents adjustments/clawbacks). Use SUM() carefully.
No fiscal year column — this is a total aggregate, not annual slices.

rcpt_st_cd.description: Prime awardee state name, Title Case. DUPLICATE of rcpt_state_name — prefer rcpt_state_name.

subawardee_st_cd.description: Sub-awardee state name, Title Case. DUPLICATE of subawardee_state_name — prefer subawardee_state_name.

rcpt_state_name.description: Full name of the prime awardee state (receives federal award and issues subawards). Title Case.

subawardee_state_name.description: Full name of the sub-awardee state (receives money downstream from prime). Title Case.

subaward_amount_year.description: Dollar amount of the subaward flow. Can be negative (adjustments). SUM() across rows for totals.

county_flow.description: Federal subaward fund flow data between U.S. counties. Each row is a prime-awardee county to sub-awardee county money flow with coordinates for mapping. Covers fiscal years 2010–2024.
county_flow.grain: One row per prime-county → sub-county flow per agency per fiscal year

rcpt_cty.description: FIPS code of the prime awardee county (receives federal award)

subawardee_cty.description: FIPS code of the sub-awardee county (receives money downstream)

rcpt_cty_name.description: Full name of prime awardee county including state. e.g. 'Montgomery County'

rcpt_state.description: State of prime awardee, Title Case

rcpt_full_name.description: Full identifier of prime awardee: 'County Name, State Name'

subawardee_full_name.description: Full identifier of sub-awardee: 'County Name, State Name'

act_dt_fis_yr.description: Federal fiscal year of the award

agency_name.description: Federal agency that issued the prime award
sample_values: Department of Health and Human Services

origin_lat.description: Latitude of prime awardee county centroid

origin_lon.description: Longitude of prime awardee county centroid

congress_flow.description: Federal subaward fund flow data between 118th Congressional Districts. Each row is a prime-awardee district to sub-awardee district money flow. Covers fiscal years 2020–2024. IMPORTANT: the district identifier columns are integers, not the standard cd_118 format — use rcpt_cd_name for readable district names.
congress_flow.grain: One row per prime-district → sub-district flow per agency per fiscal year
critical_notes: prime_awardee_stcd118 and subawardee_stcd118 are INTEGER codes (e.g. 200, 101), NOT the standard 'AK-00' / 'MD-08' format used in other tables. Do NOT join these to cd_118 columns in other tables.
Use rcpt_cd_name for human-readable district names like 'Alaska CD-00', 'Maryland CD-08'.
Unnamed: 0 is a row index — never use in queries.
naics_2digit_code is FLOAT here (not integer) due to nulls.

prime_awardee_stcd118.description: Numeric district code of prime awardee — NOT compatible with cd_118 string format in other tables. Use rcpt_cd_name for readable names.

subawardee_stcd118.description: Numeric district code of sub-awardee — NOT compatible with cd_118 string format.

rcpt_cd_name.description: Human-readable name of prime awardee district. Format: 'STATE CD-NN'. e.g. 'Maryland CD-08', 'Alaska CD-00'

rcpt_state.description: State of prime awardee, Title Case

rcpt_full_name.description: Full identifier of prime awardee district

origin_lat.description: Latitude of prime awardee district centroid

origin_lon.description: Longitude of prime awardee district centroid

naics_2digit_code.description: 2-digit NAICS industry code (stored as float due to nulls)

contract_state_agency.description: Federal spending broken down by BOTH agency AND state. Allows questions like 'how much did the Department of Defense spend in Maryland' or 'which agency gives the most grants to Texas'. 21 agencies × 50 states × 2 year-periods = 2,142 rows. Also includes employee and wage breakdown.
contract_state_agency.grain: One row per agency per state per year-period
agency_values: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development

agency.description: Federal agency name. See agency_values for exact strings.
sample_values: Department of Health and Human Services

year.description: Year period. Always quote: WHERE year = '2024'

Contracts.description: Total federal contract obligations from this agency in this geography

Grants.description: Total federal grant awards from this agency

Resident Wage.description: Total wages paid to residents tied to this agency's federal activity

Direct Payments.description: Federal direct payment transfers (Social Security, Medicare, benefits) from this agency

Federal Residents.description: Count of federal employees/residents associated with this agency in this geography

Employees.description: Federal civilian employees from this agency in this state

Employees Wage.description: Total wages paid to federal employees from this agency in this state

contract_county_agency.description: Federal spending broken down by BOTH agency AND county. Most granular agency-level dataset. 21 agencies × ~3,135 counties × 2 year-periods = ~93,700 rows. Use for questions like 'which agency gives the most grants to Montgomery County, Maryland'.
contract_county_agency.grain: One row per agency per county per year-period
agency_values: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development
contract_county_agency.critical_note: county_fips can be 1 (state-level aggregate row) or 5-digit county FIPS. Filter county_fips > 1000 for genuine county-level rows only.

agency.description: Federal agency name. See agency_values for exact strings.

county_fips.description: FIPS county code. Values of 1 are state-level aggregates — filter with county_fips > 1000 for true county rows.

Contracts.description: Total federal contract obligations from this agency in this geography

Grants.description: Total federal grant awards from this agency

Resident Wage.description: Total wages paid to residents tied to this agency's federal activity

Direct Payments.description: Federal direct payment transfers (Social Security, Medicare, benefits) from this agency

Federal Residents.description: Count of federal employees/residents associated with this agency in this geography

contract_cd_agency.description: Federal spending broken down by BOTH agency AND 118th Congressional District. 21 agencies × ~435 districts × 2 year-periods = ~18,355 rows. Use for questions like 'which congressional district gets the most Defense Department contracts'.
contract_cd_agency.grain: One row per agency per congressional district per year-period
agency_values: Department of Health and Human Services
Department of Housing and Urban Development

agency.description: Federal agency name. See agency_values for exact strings.

cd_118.description: Congressional district in standard format: STATE_ABBR-DD. e.g. 'MD-08', 'AK-00'

Contracts.description: Total federal contract obligations from this agency in this geography

Grants.description: Total federal grant awards from this agency

Resident Wage.description: Total wages paid to residents tied to this agency's federal activity

Direct Payments.description: Federal direct payment transfers (Social Security, Medicare, benefits) from this agency

Federal Residents.description: Count of federal employees/residents associated with this agency in this geography

cross_table_joins.warning: State names have THREE different casings. Always normalize with LOWER() when joining. FIPS codes are the safest join key for county-level joins.

patterns.acs_to_finra_state: JOIN on LOWER(acs_state.state) = LOWER(finra_state.state) AND acs_state.Year = finra_state.Year
patterns.acs_to_contract_state: JOIN on LOWER(acs_state.state) = LOWER(contract_state.state) — note contract year is VARCHAR
patterns.county_join_preferred: Use fips as join key for county tables: acs_county.fips = gov_county.fips = finra_county.fips
patterns.congress_join: JOIN on cd_118 directly across congress-level tables
patterns.agency_filter: Use LIKE for agency: WHERE agency LIKE '%Defense%' or exact string 'Department of Defense'

cross_table_joins.example_multi_table_query: SELECT a.state, a.financial_literacy, g.Net_Pension_Liability_per_capita FROM finra_state a JOIN gov_state g ON LOWER(a.state) = LOWER(g.state) WHERE a.Year = 2021 ORDER BY a.financial_literacy DESC
cross_table_joins.agency_tables: contract_state_agency, contract_county_agency, contract_cd_agency all have an 'agency' column — always filter with exact string e.g. WHERE agency = 'Department of Defense'
cross_table_joins.flow_to_other_tables: state_flow uses rcpt_state_name (Title Case) — join with LOWER(rcpt_state_name) = LOWER(other.state). county_flow uses integer FIPS (rcpt_cty) — join with rcpt_cty = gov_county.fips. congress_flow CANNOT be joined to other congress tables on district ID — use only rcpt_cd_name for filtering.
cross_table_joins.congress_flow_warning: congress_flow.prime_awardee_stcd118 is an INTEGER (e.g. 200), NOT the 'MD-08' string format used in all other congress tables. Never join these columns directly.

common_question_patterns.highest_lowest: Use ORDER BY col DESC/ASC LIMIT N
common_question_patterns.gov_tables_no_year_filter: gov_state/county/congress have only one year — never add a year filter
common_question_patterns.per_capita_comparison: Use _per_capita columns for fair cross-state/county comparison
common_question_patterns.finra_interpretation: Higher financial_literacy = more literate population. Higher financial_constraint = more stressed.
common_question_patterns.flow_total: SUM(subaward_amount) GROUP BY rcpt_state_name for total flow originating from each state
